what_was_built: reports/arbitrage_dataset_manifest_v1/dataset_manifest.md (human-readable: 5 per-category requirements + 32-field manifest_schema + 7-status QA model + freeze rules + identity/provenance + all field specs + allowed formats + forbidden inputs + future artifacts + PASS/WATCH/FAIL + safety boundaries + no-profit-claim policy)
tests/test_arbitrage_dataset_manifest.py (19 tests covering schema + safety + integration)
tools/strategy_candidate_registry.py: extra_files extended with the new manifest docs for the arbitrage seed (lane stays IDEA)
what_the_dataset_manifest_defines: Manifest schema with 32 required future fields every per-dataset manifest must satisfy (dataset_id, dataset_version, created_at, created_by, research_lane, arbitrage_category, venues, symbols, instruments, time_start, time_end, timezone, data_frequency, quote_type, order_book_depth, fee_schedule_reference, funding_schedule_reference, source_type, source_location, checksum_policy, row_count_expected, row_count_actual, missing_data_policy, duplicate_policy, stale_quote_policy, clock_skew_policy, normalization_policy, freeze_status, qa_status, allowed_use, forbidden_use, notes)
Per-category manifest requirements for all 5 Arbitrage Research Protocol v1 categories
Dataset identity + provenance (immutable IDs, versioning rules, source provenance, sha256 checksums, expected/actual row counts, freeze timestamps)

Data freeze rules (no mutable/unfrozen data in research; every dataset has a manifest; every manifest must validate; every dataset has a qa_status; every dataset tied to a contract version; every test cites dataset_id + dataset_version; any change -> new dataset_version; manual edits invalid unless documented + re-validated)
QA status model with 7 statuses: DRAFT, FROZEN, QA_PASS, QA_WARN, QA_FAIL, RETIRED, BLOCKED, each with exact transition semantics
Field specs: venue / instrument / time-range / timestamp / quote / order-book / trade-print / fee / funding / liquidity / latency / provenance / normalization / data-quality / freeze / validation
Allowed file formats + forbidden inputs + required future artifacts + PASS/WATCH/FAIL rules + safety boundaries + no-profit-claim policy

tests_run.command: python -m pytest tests/test_arbitrage_dataset_manifest.py tests/test_arbitrage_data_contract.py tests/test_arbitrage_research_protocol.py tests/test_strategy_candidate_registry.py tests/test_strategy_next_bundle.py --rootdir=tests -q

guardrails_held: lane is not promoted to ACTIVE just because additional docs exist
lane never reaches STRONG evidence in the auto-build path

next_bundle_generator_selection_after_up.title: Arbitrage research protocol
next_bundle_generator_selection_after_up.note: Deterministic logic NOT artificially modified. The queue at HEAD does not yet include a QA Harness Spec or Data Source Evaluation Memo item; existing protocol/data-first scoring axes still rank arbitrage at the top. Operator should consult report.md `recommended_next_bundle` for the actual Bundle 7 choice.

how_it_fits_after_arbitrage_data_contrac: Arbitrage Research Protocol v1 (30dabc4) -> Arbitrage Data Contract v1 (c4d4c8b) -> Arbitrage Dataset Manifest v1 (this bundle).
Together these three documents define WHAT arbitrage is, WHAT data is needed to test it, and HOW each real dataset must be declared/frozen/QA'd. No data has been pulled and no backtest has been run.
Subsequent bundles (QA Harness Spec, Data Source Evaluation Memo, P2 data acquisition, P3 offline replay, ...) are each separately authorized.
recommended_next_bundle: Arbitrage QA Harness Spec v1 (defines the harness that will verify any future per-dataset manifest + dataset pair; spec only, no harness run)
Arbitrage Data Source Evaluation Memo (written assessment of which offline data sources could later satisfy Categories A-E; memo only, no data pull)
Crypto-D1 Protocol Memo (alternative lane, currently PARKED; would require a new hypothesis, not a re-tune)
